text: It is designed to teach users how to do a surgeon's knot followed by two additional throws in the instrument tie fashion using the robotic arms.
The robotic arm on the left side of the screen grasps the end of the tie located on the right side of the screen.
The robotic arms initiate a double loop surgeon's knot at the wrist junction, ensuring the loops are secured to prevent slippage during the exercise.
The robotic right arm attempts to grasp a more distal portion of the tie to pull through the knot during the exercise.
The robotic arms re-grab the tie to tighten down the knot, ensuring the knot remains visible on screen.
We found the best way to do this in this exercise is to actually pull up on one end of the ties as you tighten down your knot.
The surgeon pulls up vertically on one of the ties, efficiently completing the knot and positioning the tie end for the next throw.
The surgeon completes the third and final tie using alternating hands, ensuring the knot is securely tightened.
Avoiding instrument collisions, instruments out of view, and excessive force are essential for scoring well on this exercise.
